config_meta.generated_by: authored from research/sc_recon/registry.json (Dorchester County Tier-2 recon)

records_custodian.name: Lona Conrad
records_custodian.title: Clerk to Council
mailing_address: Office of the Clerk to Council

officials_roster.notes: County Council directory (7 members). The .gov site is bot-walled (HTTP 403 to raw fetch) — needs the browser fetch tier. Verify member count + districts during the first onboarding pass.

meeting_agendas_minutes.notes: Granicus ViewPublisher hosts agendas + minutes for County Council and its committees back to 2017 (default landing view starts Dec 2019; 2017-18 via the 'Available Archives' dropdown). Agendas: AgendaViewer.php?view_id=1&event_id=<id>; minutes: /services/minutes/reports/<uuid>/attachment; packet PDFs on granicus_production_attachments.s3.amazonaws.com/dorchester/. Granicus + its S3 attachments fetch normally (outside the .gov bot wall). Granicus lists ~13 meeting bodies (Council + committees + Special/Emergency/Joint variants) — a per-body scraper must enumerate all of them, not a fixed 9.

records_request.notes: NextRequest FOIA portal (outside the .gov bot wall). SC FOIA (S.C. Code 30-4-30(C)): the body must determine within 10 business days (20 if the record is >24 months old); records furnished within 30 calendar days of deposit. FOIA Coordinator in the County Attorney's Office, 500 N. Main St., Summerville SC 29483.

campaign_finance.notes: SC centralizes candidate campaign-disclosure filings at the State Ethics Commission (S.C. Code 8-13-100(2) / 8-13-365) — county candidates file there, not with a local clerk.

public_comment.notes: Citizens addressing agenda items complete the online 'Request to Address Council' form (administered by the Clerk to Council); the exact form URL sits behind the .gov bot wall (not captured). Council meets 1st & 3rd Mondays at 6:00 PM (one meeting only in Aug & Dec). Meetings ROTATE venues between St. George (Willie R. Davis County Council Chambers, 201 Johnston St) and Summerville (500 N. Main St) — single-address automation is wrong.

known_gaps.explanation: The official .gov (dorchestercountysc.gov, Granicus govAccess CMS) returns HTTP 403 to raw fetching — nav/listing pages need the browser fetch tier. The agenda/minutes platform (Granicus), the NextRequest FOIA portal, and Granicus S3 attachments sit OUTSIDE the wall and fetch normally.
known_gaps.user_facing_message: Some county pages load through a verification step; agendas, minutes, and records requests are unaffected.
known_gaps.remediation: Use the browser fetch tier for .gov nav pages; prefer the Granicus + NextRequest sources.

known_gaps.explanation: Dorchester County has two independent unified school districts — Dorchester School District Two (Summerville) and Dorchester School District Four (St. George) — each its own elected Board of Trustees. They bundle to Dorchester County for funding but are separate type=school_district jurisdictions.
known_gaps.user_facing_message: Dorchester's two school districts are separate jurisdictions — their records live at their own pages when added.
known_gaps.remediation: Onboard dorchester-school-district-two-sc and dorchester-school-district-four-sc as separate jurisdictions (both bundle_fips = 45035).

known_gaps.explanation: Sheriff, Coroner, and other constitutional officers run separate records-request pages and are individually elected — not council bodies. Not modeled here.
known_gaps.user_facing_message: Pages for the Sheriff, Coroner, and other elected officers are not yet indexed.
known_gaps.remediation: Add a constitutional-officer model (shared with the GA constitutional-officers gap).
